articles.title: Origin Lab raises $8M to help video game companies sell data to world-model builders
articles.description: Origin Lab will serve as a marketplace where AI labs can buy high-quality licensed data, and video-game companies can sell it.

articles.title: Anthropic courts a new kind of customer: small business owners
articles.description: Anthropic is looking to broaden its customer base from larger enterprise customers to smaller and mid-sized businesses.

articles.title: Ransomware hackers claim breach at Foxconn, a major electronics manufacturer for Apple, Google, and Nvidia
articles.description: A ransomware group has claimed responsibility for hacking the electronics manufacturing giant Foxconn, and is attempting to extort the company.

articles.title: Amazon launches an AI shopping assistant for the search bar, powered by Alexa+
articles.description: Alexa for Shopping is a new personalized AI shopping assistant in the Amazon search bar that replaces its Rufus assistant.

articles.title: Anduril raises $5B, doubles valuation to $61B
articles.description: After achieving $2.2 billion in revenue in 2025, the defense tech startup has raised another massive round, led by Thrive and a16z, it says.

articles.title: r/WallStreetBets really hates the SEC’s proposal to weaken quarterly reporting
articles.description: The retail trading subreddit submitted the sharpest criticism yet in response to the financial regulator's idea of letting companies report twice per year.

articles.title: US lawmakers demand answers from Instructure after Canvas data breaches
articles.description: U.S. House lawmakers want to know how hackers broke into education tech giant Instructure twice, and stole reams of data from students who use the company's flagship student data software Canvas.

articles.title: Anthropic now has more business customers than OpenAI, according to Ramp data
articles.description: For the first time, Anthropic has more verified business customers than OpenAI, according to this month’s AI Index from the fintech firm Ramp.

articles.title: Introducing the 6 stages at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 — built for today’s tougher startup market
articles.description: From October 13-15, TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 will feature 200+ sessions across six stages, led by 250+ tech leaders shaping the industry today. Register now to save up to $410, plus 50% off a second pass.

articles.title: WhatsApp adds an incognito mode in Meta AI chats
articles.description: Meta said these incognito conversations are not saved, and messages will disappear by default once you close the chat.

articles.title: Poppy debuts a proactive AI assistant to help organize your digital life
articles.description: Poppy is an AI-powered app that connects your calendar, email, messages, and other services to surface reminders, suggestions, and tasks based on what’s happening in your life.

articles.title: Adaption aims big with AutoScientist, an AI tool that helps models train themselves
articles.description: Adaption's new AutoScientist tool is designed to let models adapt to specific capabilities quickly through an automated approach to conventional fine-tuning.

articles.title: Medicare’s new payment model is built for AI, and most of the tech world has no idea
articles.description: There is no governmental mechanism to pay for an AI agent that monitors a patient between visits, calls to check in, coordinates a housing referral, or makes sure someone picks up their medication. ACCESS creates that mechanism for the first time.

articles.title: Kevin Hartz’s A* just closed its third fund with $450M
articles.description: The firm takes a generalist approach, backing companies across categories such as AI applications, fintech, healthcare, and security. The average check size for this fund will be between $3 million and $5 million, with the aim to back at least 30 startups.

articles.title: Former Tesla exec and Heron Power CEO Drew Baglino has founded a heat pump startup
articles.description: Sadi Thermal Machines is Drew Baglino's second startup since leaving Tesla in 2024.

articles.title: Musk mulled handing OpenAI to his children, Altman testifies
articles.description: Altman said that Musk's focus on controlling the initial for-profit gave him pause because OpenAI was dedicated to keeping advanced AI out of the hands of a single person, and Altman, with his experience running the prominent startup accelerator Y Combinator, knew "founders who had control usually did not give it up."

articles.title: Anthropic warns investors against secondary platforms offering access to its shares
articles.description: "Any sale or transfer of Anthropic stock, or any interest in Anthropic stock, offered by these firms is void and will not be recognized on our books and records," the company's support page reads.

articles.title: Potholes cost cities millions: This company is using AI and trucks to fix them
articles.description: Fleet management company Samsara has developed an AI model to detect different kinds of potholes and gauge how fast they're deteriorating.

articles.title: Report: Google and SpaceX in talks to put data centers into orbit
articles.description: Google and SpaceX are in talks to build data centers in orbit, pitching space as the future home for AI compute, even as costs today remain far higher than on the ground.

articles.title: Everything Google announced at its Android Show, from Googlebooks to vibe-coded widgets
articles.description: Google unveiled its new AI-first Googlebooks laptops, more agentic Gemini features, vibe-coded Android widgets, Gemini in Chrome, refreshed Android Auto, and more ahead of I/O.

articles.title: Gravitational lens shows a galaxy just 800 million years post-Big Bang
articles.description: Early galaxy has elements produced by the Universe's first supernovae.

articles.title: Blue Origin may need external funding to hit ambitious launch targets
articles.description: Are the pockets of Jeff Bezos not as deep as everyone thinks?

articles.description: eBay board doubts GameStop's ability to buy and operate the much larger firm.

articles.title: Google's Android-powered laptops are called Googlebooks, and they're coming this year

articles.description: Google has big plans for Android in 2026, and most of it is AI.

articles.title: After banning foreign routers, FCC says existing ones can get updates until 2029
articles.description: FCC extends waiver allowing routers and drones to get patches for two more years.

articles.title: Data center guzzled 30 million gallons of water, and nobody noticed for months
articles.description: Can AI save us from the AI industry’s endless thirst for water? Outlook not so good.

articles.title: Passengers from hantavirus ship arrive in US; 3 people in biocontainment
articles.description: A US passenger tested "mildly positive," but WHO is calling it "inconclusive" for now.

tech.summary:
今日科技动态摘要

1. **AI与数据应用加速**：Origin Lab获800万美元融资，帮助游戏公司向世界模型构建者销售数据；Adaption推出AutoScientist，一款让模型自我训练的AI工具；Poppy发布主动式AI助手，协助管理数字生活。
2. **企业AI竞争格局变化**：Anthropic积极拓展小企业客户，且据Ramp数据显示，其商业客户数已超过OpenAI；亚马逊推出由Alexa+驱动的AI购物助手，嵌入搜索栏。
3. **网络安全与数据泄露**：勒索软件黑客声称入侵富士康（苹果、谷歌、英伟达的主要代工商）；美国议员就Canvas数据泄露事件向Instructure施压。
4. **融资与市场动态**：Anduril融资50亿美元，估值翻倍至610亿美元；Kevin Hartz的A*完成第四支基金募集，规模4.5亿美元；前特斯拉高管Drew Baglino创办热泵初创公司。
5. **监管与用户隐私**：华尔街赌注社区强烈反对SEC削弱季度报告制度的提案；WhatsApp在Meta AI聊天中新增“隐身模式”。

今日最值得关注的发展是：AI正从“辅助工具”加速向“数据驱动模型自训练”和“主动管理数字生活”演进，同时企业AI服务市场的竞争格局正在被Anthropic等新势力重塑。

ai.summary:
今日AI动态摘要

1. **AWS与Hugging Face合作推进基础模型训练与推理**：聚焦于在AWS上构建和部署基础模型的技术框架，并探讨了混合专家模型（MoE）预训练方法，以提升模型的模块化与效率。
2. **Google AI在金融与广告领域的应用扩展**：AI驱动的Google Finance服务向欧洲市场扩张；同时展示创意人士利用AI为小企业制作广告的案例，强调AI在商业场景中的实际价值。
3. **OpenAI多领域实践与安全部署**：涵盖Codex在金融团队、NVIDIA工程师及研究中的应用，以及ChatGPT在2026年初的普及趋势；同时推出DeployCo服务帮助企业围绕AI构建业务，并强调Codex的安全运行与GPT-5.5在网络安全领域的可信访问扩展。

**今日最值得关注的发展**：OpenAI正式推出DeployCo，旨在帮助企业规模化部署AI智能，标志着AI从技术工具向企业级服务生态的关键转型。

cctv.summary:
今日新闻联播动态摘要

1. **国际关系与外交动态**：美国多位企业领袖（如马斯克、黄仁勋）随特朗普访华，同时美国议员卢比奥通过语言技术手段获准赴华，中方在会谈前批评美国芯片设备法案，显示中美高层互动与摩擦并存。  
2. **涉华争议事件**：美国加州一市长因承认“作为中国代理人”而辞职，西方媒体借此炒作“中国渗透”议题，中方尚未正式回应。  
3. **历史与意识形态议题**：外媒报道东京审判揭露靖国神社歪曲历史叙事，强调中国在历史正义问题上的立场。  
4. **科技与产业进展**：中国Unitree公司量产可骑乘变形机器人GD01（售价53.7万美元），中方同时承认对新技术的治理比研发更具挑战。  
5. **全球影响力与舆论**：中国在全球民调中支持率超过美国，但外媒称美国国际开发署（USAID）被削弱后中国未填补其空缺，暗示中方在软实力领域的策略性克制。  

**总结**：今日焦点围绕中美博弈的多维度展开——从高层互访、技术竞争到舆论战，同时中国在历史叙事和前沿科技领域持续输出自身主张。
